Step 4: Bring your arm back, without your palm facing up. This will give the beanbag a pull. Make sure you keep the same angle as the previous step.

Step 5: Swing your arm forward in the direction you want the beanbag to go. You will hear it go “pop”.

Step 6: Repeat

Estonia was heavily influenced by musical German traditions. The first foundations of the high-culture development were laid in 1749–59 when the foundation of the Estonian University made it possible to establish a high school with a musical department.

After 1815, with the re-establishment of the independence, the music activity gradually was enhanced. The influential intellectual figures in the life of the Estonian nation, like Kristjan Jaak Peterson, Johann Voldemar Jannsen, Karl Gottlieb von Sievers, Johannes Aavik and Theodor Kull were composers and organists. Many of the composers (Krull, Sell, Sellmann) were Baltic German.

The first Estonian concert took place in 1846.  Since 1834 there were also courses in the Tartu Lyceum and in 1868 a regular music class. In Tartu there was a orchestra in the basement of the university in 1865 and the music professor Anton Schuberth, who founded in 1869 the first professional orchestra in Estonia, consisting of his own students.

Concert-tourists of the von Sievers family founded the first concert societies in St. Petersburg, Stockholm and Copenhagen in 1852. It was also in their family that a Estonian concert tradition was established.

In 1851 the first national song festival was organized by Väino ja Karl Uuttu. This song festival was a part of the preparations for the Estonian Song Festival as it was established in 1869.

The first symphonic work in Estonian music, the overture Julius Caesar by Rudolf Tobias, was published in 1896.

Human: Great. Could you also give a brief history of Arvo Pärt and his development as a composer?

Arvo Pärt (born April 11, 1935) is an Estonian composer. Pärt is widely regarded as one of the most significant composers of the late 20th century.[1][2][3] After a period of study in Moscow in 1959, where he initially fell under the influence of the conservatory, Pärt returned to his native Tallinn and from 1967 began composing music that has since earned him worldwide acclaim.

Born in Paide, Pärt trained as a piano and violin player. In 1955 he became a pupil of Neeme Järvi at the Estonian State Conservatory, a decision influenced by his wish to distance himself from the Soviet musical establishment. In 1958, Pärt became a teacher of piano at Tallinn's Viru Gildi Kool, but in 1959 he enrolled at the Moscow Conservatory in order to study composition.

By 1964 Pärt had been accepted by Sergei Prokofiev as one of the young Soviet composers most likely to succeed him. In 1967 he took up a post as a professor of composition at the Conservatory in Tallinn. During the late 1960s and 1970s Pärt's compositional style began to change. His pieces started to feature Orthodox hymn tunes in an asymmetrical setting. His pieces, which he referred to as "tintinnabuli", were also characterized by a melodic recitative-like quality and dense, intricate patterns. Pärt described this period of his compositional life as the beginning of his own spiritual journey.

By the mid-1970s, Pärt had completed his first mature masterpiece, the 11-minute Gregorian Toccata, a minimalist-style piece which incorporated fragments of Gregorian plainchant.

In September 2000, Arvo Pärt's work "Fratres" (meaning "Brothers") was voted the best classical work of the 20th century. In 2005, his "Te Deum" was voted the Best Composition of the last 50 years.

Assistant:
response:
OK, here's how to make it:

Lapskaus is a cold dish made of reindeer or other lean meat, and potatoes, served with a bechamel sauce.  The meat is chopped into small pieces and left to sit in a bag, either in the refrigerator or freezer, for at least 4 hours.  The bag should be kept cold to keep the meat from spoiling, and it must be removed from the bag and scraped out before serving.

Potatoes are boiled until soft, then mashed and strained.  Mashed cooked carrots are added, along with dried dill and flour.  Next, a cream sauce is made by melting butter in a pot, adding flour and cream, and cooking to a thick sauce.  Lapskaus is then layered into serving dishes, with the bechamel sauce on top.  It can be garnished with parsley and snipped chives, or grated cheese.

First, thawing in the refrigerator is preferable to thawing on the counter, because bacteria and mold can develop.

These steps are to remove any bacteria and moisture that could collect in the cavity, and cause any salmonella or other harmful bacteria to grow. Then, after it is defrosted and patted dry, it is best to place the turkey in a container of cold water and ice, and keep the temperature of that ice bath below 40 degrees fahrenheit. Assistant: Sure, black holes are regions of space so dense that nothing, not even light, can escape from within them. Not even the most powerful telescope can detect the photons emitted by matter falling into a black hole, because they don’t reach the event horizon. The mass of a black hole can be as high as ten billion suns, and at the event horizon the gravitational field is so strong that a stone falling into the hole would have more than half the speed of light at impact.

Human: How does a black hole form?

Assistant: One way a black hole is formed is by the gravitational collapse of a supermassive star. This can happen when an extremely large star has run out of nuclear fuel and is burning its inner core. The star will collapse as the outer layers of gas stream away into space. Once the core starts to collapse the density and temperature in the core rapidly increases, this creates a high pressure so that the core implodes to form a superdense star, known as a neutron star. However this star is not a completely spherical object, and instead has a dense central core that can reach a density of three million times the density of lead, where even neutrons are crushed to become quarks. However the material in the core doesn’t have enough pressure to stop the collapse and to prevent it from continuing. As the core continues to collapse, the temperature increases to over 100 trillion Kelvin and the density approaches the Planck density. At this density the strong force of attraction can not prevent the core from continuing to collapse until it forms a black hole, and so at that point the matter in the core is no longer able to resist gravity and instead collapses to form a black hole.

Human: What is the planck density?

response: The Planck density is the density at which the quantum gravitational forces of attraction on a given subatomic particle would equal the strength of the gravity produced by that particle, which is believed to be the limit of the mass of any object in our universe.

rejected: The Planck density is a very high density that is defined by the Planck constant. A particle that is at the Planck density is smaller than the wavelength of light, and so we cannot see it with current technology.

4 eggs

Pareikštas is Lithuanian word that means a little bit. In this case it means a single dumpling, but it can also be used to make groups of similar dumplings. These dumplings are boiled and then added to soup and eaten.

* 200ml dry white wine

* 3 large eggs

rejected: Right, that was the popular name given to a specific sort of vulnerability in computer code.  The potential problem was this: computer software often contained date values using just the last two digits of the year.  So for instance, year 2000 in "base 10" would be expressed as "00".  Year 2100 in base 10 would be expressed as "11".  So if a computer's date check uses just the last two digits of the year, then it would technically allow a date to occur during the "Y2K" era!  So people knew that if a specific computer had this kind of software error, a critical flaw would be introduced into its operation, because its program would allow a fake date.  People also knew that during the "Y2K" era, computers would automatically be upgraded to "Y2K-safe" software, and so then this flaw would have been remedied.  And some other people thought that perhaps a few very old computers would be unable to handle the change to the new date format, and would malfunction.  So some of these people decided to make the date change a big public issue, and to publish articles and give talks, and even to cause some Y2K panic.

1 cup flour
1 tablespoon of oil (any oil will do)
water as needed
a rolling pin

Once the dough is in this form, you can then pat it out until it’s around half a centimeter thick, and use a knife to cut it into triangles. You can then cook the triangles on a griddle or skillet, which will help to create a chewy texture inside the chapati.

rejected:

The basic idea is to create an environment that will attract different types of soil-eating worms, so they can feed on your food scraps.  Different soil-eating worms are adapted to different types of food scraps, so you have to introduce several different kinds of worms, and design your environment so they can eat your food scraps without becoming cannibals.  

In an ideal world, you’ll get enough worms that they’ll “recycle” your scraps and don’t create any garbage.  You can grow plants that attract worms, and their waste will become soil.  But you should also make sure you’re giving the worms an environment that’s too hot or too wet, so they can’t reproduce too much and end up rotting your food scraps.

It works by measuring your blood pressure, breathing, and heart rate, and analyzing those signals in response to a series of questions, and then interpreting the measurements.  How reliable are polygraphs, then?  Well, some are better than others, but in general, they’re about 80 to 90 percent accurate.

Human: Are there any ways of tricking them?

Assistant: Yes, there are various things that can make polygraphs seem to give inaccurate results.  For instance, if the person undergoing the test has extreme levels of anxiety about the question, the test could give false negatives (which means the test didn’t indicate that the person is lying).  
Other factors that might make the polygraph seem inaccurate include distracting noises (perhaps you have to take the test at a police station, and you can hear people talking or shouting in the background), cold air (since a polygraph is a kind of physical stress test, it’s better to be warmed up for it), and even thoughts of small mammals and other distracting subjects.  
If these things are taken into account, then a polygraph is about 93 to 95 percent accurate.  This means that a polygraph is pretty reliable for differentiating actual lying from innocent false negatives, but if you know what kinds of factors can cause problems for a polygraph, it’s fairly easy to fool it.

Assistant:
response: That’s certainly possible, and this is why polygraphs should be administered in a controlled environment, with no distractions, and with the consent of all parties.  By doing this, you can ensure the best accuracy in the test.

Police departments are under pressure to make polygraph results sound compelling in court, so if someone really wanted to make a polygraph seem inaccurate, they could take advantage of those pressures.  
A group of people might also just hire a polygrapher with a bad reputation or a bad set of equipment, so that it would come up as saying the person is lying when they weren’t, in hopes of obtaining a false conviction.  
That said, even a person who knows the best ways to get around the problems with the polygraph might not want to try to fool it for long.  A polygraph test takes a long time, and it’s easy to become anxious during the test, causing your actual emotions to leak into the data. A heart attack occurs when your heart muscle suddenly becomes injured.  The muscle cells of your heart start dying off, and you're no longer able to pump blood to your body.  If the heart muscle is not able to pump blood to your brain and other vital organs, you can end up with a stroke.  This can cause permanent brain damage or death.

There's also a higher risk of heart disease in people with high blood pressure.  People with high blood pressure are also more likely to have a stroke or heart attack.  Other lifestyle factors also contribute to heart disease, including obesity, lack of exercise, high blood sugar, or diabetes.

The seasons during which monsoon systems occur are spring and summer, typically April through June, and also fall and winter, typically October through December. In some areas, like India, they happen with some regularity every year, and in other areas, they happen in some years, and not in others.  The Pacific Northwest, for example, gets monsoon-like weather every year, but monsoons can also arrive and depart from year to year.

Human: How are typhoons formed?

Assistant: They’re formed when the ocean heats up in a spiral pattern.  When the water temperature goes over 86°F, the ocean is too warm for there to be as many clouds or thunderstorms as there normally are.  So a lot of water evaporates and turns into a hot, rising layer of air, which cools down once it gets into the upper atmosphere.  That cooling process creates a lot of condensation, which forms clouds, which in turn create rain and snow.  And rain can cause runoff, which causes the ocean to rise and lower, which creates stronger winds and can lead to flooding.  When the winds grow strong enough, they can reach hurricane strength, even if the ocean temperature hasn’t increased all that much.  The hurricane then grows to a typhoon.

Assistant: We don’t know much about the inner workings of hurricanes or typhoons, but it is hypothesized that the temperature difference from cold to warm water (called thermal gradient) can produce strong winds.  Air can be warm or cold when it rises up from the ocean or falls down towards it, and so the updraft or downdraft can become a strong wind and cause rotation.  That rotation is thought to create the strong winds of hurricanes and typhoons.

Human: I see. How then does a typhoon travel from one point to another?

Assistant: They move by getting hit with winds that push them in that direction.  When they get really strong, these winds can have the power to create waves that can even reach up to 30 feet high.  Once a typhoon travels far enough from the ocean, the winds and waves die down because there isn’t much water to continue to act as a pressure source.  But we still sometimes get big waves because of the strong winds and the swirling ocean currents.  And the tornadoes and hailstorms that form in strong winds can often travel hundreds of miles.

In the Northern Hemisphere, where most typhoons are, the direction of the wind is counterclockwise, so the spiral arms go clockwise, and so it looks like the wind is just moving in a circle.

rejected: There are two kinds of typhoons.  The ones in the Western Pacific and Northwest Pacific are called “meso-scale” and they move at speeds under 5 knots or less. prompt:
Human: How do I keep ants from coming in the house?

Assistant: Ants are attracted to sweet and damp foods, and to stored water.  So it’s good to stop food and water from going outside.  This can be as simple as closing doors and windows that you have a screen for, or if not, stopping up any holes or cracks in the siding.  

Once you’ve stopped the food and water from going outside, you should clean up spilled food and used dishes.  This will stop ants from coming in to look for the food that has already been spilled.

Human: Any other advice?

Assistant: If you notice a pattern of ants coming in a particular location, try sealing up that area by taping plastic over any holes in the floor.  If you find an existing colony, you may want to clean up its food and water supply, then spray insecticide inside the house and outside around the house to kill the ants.  Be sure to follow label instructions when using insecticide.  Once you have done this, you can return to the house for a few hours and look for any ants inside, to make sure you’ve gotten all the workers and larvae that you can find.  Ants that you do not find, or ones that return, will go out looking for food and water outside again.  You may need to repeat this process to completely get rid of them.

prompt:
Human: What medicines are available to treat anxiety

Assistant:
response:
There are two primary categories of anxiety medications: behavioral therapy and drugs.

Behavioral therapy may help some people manage anxiety. It involves identifying and changing behaviors that may contribute to anxiety, as well as changing your attitude and beliefs about anxiety. Therapies like CBT (cognitive behavioral therapy), DBT (dialectical behavioral therapy), and mindfulness-based stress reduction have been shown to help some people manage anxiety. Exercise also helps some people feel more calm. In addition, various types of mindfulness practices can help you feel more calm or grounded. Most people benefit from some combination of behavioral therapy and exercise.

Several medications are used to treat anxiety. The medications may work by increasing serotonin levels in the brain, decreasing serotonin levels in the brain, or they may work by inhibiting the reuptake of certain neurotransmitters such as GABA, or they may be anti-anxiety medications that work in a variety of ways. Certain medications may also help with sleep if you are dealing with insomnia.

Common types of medications used to treat anxiety include SSRIs such as fluoxetine (Prozac, Rexulti, Sarafem), citalopram (Celexa), escitalopram (Lexapro), and sertraline (Zoloft), and SNRIs such as venlafaxine (Effexor, Efexor XR), duloxetine (Cymbalta), and mirtazapine (Remeron, Norpramine). They may also be used in combination with other medications. Examples include bupropion (Wellbutrin), buspirone (Buspar), clonidine (Klonopin), and many others. Some people may also take supplements like kava or magnesium.

For many people, anxiety medications help them manage their anxiety. These medications are not appropriate for everyone, and they may be associated with some risks. In some people, they can cause sleepiness or drowsiness, or they can cause some irritability. Some people may be more likely to experience side effects than others. Additionally, these medications can only treat one aspect of anxiety. They may help with anxiety symptoms, but they may not address other issues that some people with anxiety face, such as other health problems, mental health issues, or other issues in their life. If you are considering using a medication to help manage your anxiety, you should discuss the risks and benefits of these medications with your healthcare provider.
